title: scolymus hispanicus subsp. hispanicus
intro: Scolymus hispanicus subsp. hispanicus, commonly known as the Spanish artichoke or artichoke thistle, is a perennial plant in the sunflower family, characterized by its edible buds and thistle-like appearance. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral effects, which help to protect against cell damage and promote overall health. The health benefits of Scolymus hispanicus subsp. hispanicus include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a valuable addition to a healthy diet and lifestyle. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are typically dried and used in teas, infusions, and supplements to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
